summary.intent: Fix an AI metrics dashboard where BMAD Command Frequency and many other charts were empty or showing incorrect data, then add a checkpoint selector for filtering dashboard data.
summary.outcome: All major charts were fixed with real data, a checkpoint selector was implemented, and several data interpretation bugs (attribution inversion, wrong turn counts, missing BMAD commands) were resolved. Dashboard now shows 9 checkpoints with correct per-checkpoint filtering.

repo: Claude Code JSONL format stores user prompt at `parsed.message.content`, not `parsed.content`; slash commands are wrapped in `<command-name>/bmad-dev-story</command-name>` XML tags, not bare strings

Checkpoint branch layout is `<2-char-prefix>/<checkpoint-id>/` with per-session subdirectories (`/0/`, `/1/`, etc.) each containing `metadata.json` and `full.jsonl` — not a flat `checkpoints/<id>/metadata.json` structure
`initial_attribution` in session metadata is a cumulative repo-wide snapshot, not a per-checkpoint delta — `human_added: 708,268` means total repo human lines, not lines added in that checkpoint
Model info (`message.model`) is only in JSONL transcripts on assistant messages, not in session `metadata.json`
GitHub unauthenticated API rate limit is 60 req/hour; fetching JSONL transcripts for all sessions across 9 checkpoints exceeds this, causing silent failures for later checkpoints
Checkpoints without session subdirectories have epoch dates (1970) and all-zero data — these must be filtered out before rendering
Peak `agent_percentage` across sessions is more reliable than last-session attribution because humans edit agent code between sessions, reclassifying attribution downward

code.finding: extractBmadCommands had wrong content path (parsed.content vs parsed.message.content) and wrong regex (/^\/[a-z]/ missed XML-wrapped commands)
BMAD Command Frequency used DARK_CHART_OPTIONS spread for horizontal bar chart — the shared scales.y config forced linear scale with yMax:1 on a category axis, making all bars invisible
Session Depth scatter chart used turns.length (session count: 1-5) instead of sum(turn_count) for x-axis — actual API calls range 2-70
API Call Count chart also used turns.length (session count) instead of sum(turn_count)
Initial SSR-created Chart.js instances were not destroyed before re-render on checkpoint selection; needed to call Chart.getChart(canvas) and destroy before creating new instance

workflow: Astro's bundler processes `<script type="application/json">` blocks — use data attributes on HTML elements instead to embed JSON for client-side JS
When GitHub rate limit is hit mid-fetch, errors are silent and later checkpoints get empty BMAD/model/prompt data; local JSONL files can be used to patch the cache
Chart.js horizontal bar charts (indexAxis: 'y') require explicit scale configuration and cannot safely inherit shared options designed for vertical charts

friction: GitHub unauthenticated API rate limit (60 req/hour) caused multiple blocked fetch attempts and silent data gaps — required waiting ~47 minutes between attempts
Cache patching from local JSONL files was needed repeatedly because remote fetches kept hitting rate limits mid-execution
Attribution data was fundamentally misleading (cumulative repo snapshots vs per-checkpoint deltas) — required redefining the chart metric entirely rather than fixing a code bug
Astro bundler silently broke `<script type="application/json">` data embedding, causing a JSON parse error only visible in the browser console
open_items: Model Distribution and Prompt Type Distribution still require a re-fetch with a GitHub PAT or after rate limit reset to get data from GitHub-hosted JSONL transcripts (currently patched from local files only)

Friction Count, Open Items Over Time, and Learnings per Checkpoint charts were removed because the data fields don't exist in the checkpoint format — if these metrics become available in the future they would need to be re-added
Fetch script hits GitHub rate limit for repos with many checkpoints/sessions — should implement batching, caching of individual JSONL files, or require a PAT for large repos
4 checkpoints without session subdirectories are silently filtered out — root cause (why they exist with no sessions) is unknown and may indicate a data pipeline issue upstream
